steps.description: Click the "Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu at coordinates (394, 85).
Click the "Create declarative agent only" option within the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown field in the Workspace Folder selection window to specify the project location.
Click the "Provision" option located under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Type the text 'notifications: Show notifications' in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette to search for the 'Notifications: Show Notifications' command.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" option in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.
@assertion there's provision stage executed successfully.
Click the "Share" option located in the "LIFECYCLE" section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar to open the shared scope configuration panel.
Click the "Share access" dropdown option in the "Share the agent" dialog of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Share the agent with selected users" dropdown option within the "Select a sharing scope" list in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Type the email address #text:'${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}' into the 'Email addresses of users or groups for agent sharing' field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Press the 'Enter' key in the input field labeled "Email addresses of users or groups for agent sharing" to confirm the entered email address: `${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}`.
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code window, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
@assertion Agent successfully shared with users:
Click the "Share" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface to change the shared scope of the agent.
Click the "Remove access for selected user(s)" option from the dropdown menu under "Share the agent."
Click on the input field labeled "Email addresses of users or groups for agent access removal" near the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Type the email address #text:'${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}' into the input field labeled 'Email addresses of users or groups for agent access removal' within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Press 'Enter' to confirm the email address in the "Email addresses of users or groups for agent access removal" field within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code window, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
@assertion Shared access successfully removed from users:
Click the "Share" button in the Lifecycle section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar to change the shared scope of the agent.
Click the "Share access with selected user(s)" option in the dropdown menu under the "Share the agent" search field within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the dropdown option "Share the agent with all tenant users" in the "Select a sharing scope" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "dev" environment option in the "Select an environment" dropdown dialog within Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
@assertion Agent successfully shared with tenant.
